name: Huidong Lin
summary: Climate and sustainable trade professional with experience in green trade policy, market intelligence, stakeholder coordination, and cross-border sustainability initiatives. Skilled in policy research, carbon market analysis, executive briefing, and program support across international nonprofit and professional service contexts.

experience.title: Specialist, Sustainable Trade
bullets: Supported sustainable trade initiatives focused on climate policy, green supply chains, carbon markets, and cross-border low-carbon transition topics.
Prepared policy research, market updates, and briefing materials for internal teams and external stakeholders.
Tracked developments in green trade, carbon border measures, voluntary carbon markets, and related regulatory discussions.
Coordinated meetings, project timelines, and knowledge products across policy, business, and research partners.

experience.title: Consultant
bullets: Conducted market research, competitor analysis, and data synthesis for client strategy and operations projects.
Built Excel-based analysis and presentation materials to support client decision-making.
Worked with cross-functional project teams to deliver client-ready reports under tight timelines.

education.degree: Master of International Public Policy; completed alongside full-time professional work

education.school: University of International Business and Economics

skills.skills: Climate policy research; green trade; carbon markets; market intelligence; Microsoft Office Suite; Excel-based data analysis; Asana
